access.anon: Can read homework information under public sections, but cannot create, update, or delete.
access.user: When signed in and not suspended, can create and update homework; delete permission is limited to the creator and admins; each user can only manage their own homework completion state.
access.admin: Can delete homework and participate in governance.
access.agent: After authorization, can read, create, update, and delete permitted homework and set the current user's completion state; REST, GraphQL, and MCP provide the supported business mutations.

rules.attached-to-section: Homework is attached to a section, not a personal user todo.
rules.no-subscription-required: Creating homework does not require the user to be subscribed to the section first.
rules.entity-and-completion-separated: Homework entity and homework completion state are strictly separated to prevent 'I completed it' from becoming 'the homework was modified'.
rules.mobile-toolbar-priority: At mobile widths, completion-status filters stay directly available, card/list display mode moves into an accessible overflow menu, and the create action remains a minimum 44 by 44 CSS pixel target. Desktop toolbar behavior remains unchanged.
rules.compact-card-list-surface: In card and list views, the default homework surface only shows title, subtitle (course name when useful plus non-default attribute badges), submission due date, relative due label, and a small completion action. Standard/default homework is not shown as a separate badge. Course/section context, description, homework timestamps, discussion, and secondary actions belong in a centered detail popup that opens on click and closes via outside click or Escape.
rules.homework-style-guide-advisory: Create and edit surfaces expose a collapsible homework style guide. Titles use 第{N}次作业 or {主题}作业 without course name/code, and explicitly avoid chapter-only titles such as 第一章作业. Descriptions list 题目, 提交方式, 提交地址, and 备注 in that order; 题目 uses 未记录 when unknown and unknown optional fields are omitted. The guide is advisory only and never blocks otherwise valid writes.

routes.returns: { viewer, homeworks: HomeworkItem[], auditLogs: AuditLog[], sectionIds: Int[] }
notes: Returns homeworks across all subscribed sections in a single call; includes audit logs and viewer context.
REST localizes returned homework relation fields from the request locale; MCP accepts an explicit locale input.

fields.name: homeworks
notes: Inputs expose completion, due-time, semester, and bounded PageInput filters only; section ownership is relation-derived.

tools.name: list_my_homeworks
notes: Returns homeworks[]; each item contains title, submissionDueAt, section.course.namePrimary, section.code, description, and completion.
Omitting semesterId reads all followed semesters; pass a semesterId to recover one current or past term.

fields: mobile compact toolbar with direct completion filters and overflow display-mode control
cards/list view mode persisted in browser storage
detail popup order: description, due summary, vertical metadata excluding platform createdAt, action controls, discussion; desktop places discussion to the right of the details
create form exposes the collapsible advisory homework title and description style guide

routes.returns: { viewer: ViewerContext, homeworks: HomeworkItem[], auditLogs: AuditLog[] }
notes: Accepts sectionJwId to match the MCP tool's public section identifier, while preserving sectionId and sectionIds for existing REST clients.
REST localizes returned homework relation fields from the request locale; MCP accepts an explicit locale input.
Returns the created homework snapshot so REST and MCP clients can continue with follow-up actions without an extra list call.
Returns the updated homework snapshot after metadata and/or description changes are applied.

mutations.name: createHomework
notes: Accepts the public sectionJwId, returns the localized Homework snapshot, and preserves the shared created HomeworkAuditLog transaction.

mutations.name: updateHomework
notes: Nullable description and timestamp fields clear existing values; omitted fields remain unchanged.
Description changes preserve the shared DescriptionEdit and description_edit audit transaction.

mutations.name: deleteHomework
notes: Only the creator or an admin may delete; repeated deletion remains successful with alreadyDeleted=true and does not add another deletion audit row.

tools.name: list_homeworks_by_section
tools.returns: { found: Boolean, section: Section?, homeworks: HomeworkItem[] }
notes: Default and the deprecated summary alias omit the repeated per-item section payload because the top-level section already scopes the list.
Editor identity fields stay in full mode; default focuses on homework state and due context.

tools.name: create_homework_on_section
tools.returns: { success: Boolean, id: String, homework: HomeworkItem }
notes: REST accepts either sectionId for compatibility or sectionJwId to match the MCP tool's public section identifier; when both are supplied, they must resolve to the same section or the request returns 400.
Returns the created homework snapshot so MCP clients can continue with follow-up actions without an extra read.
Description advertises the advisory 第{N}次作业 / {主题}作业 title patterns and ordered 题目 / 提交方式 / 提交地址 / 备注 template; formatting is not validated or enforced.

tools.name: update_homework_on_section
notes: Accepts locale for the returned homework snapshot, matching create/list localization.
Returns the updated homework snapshot after metadata and/or description changes are applied.
Description advertises the same advisory title and ordered description convention as create_homework_on_section; nonconforming existing or updated values remain accepted.

tools.name: delete_homework_on_section
tools.returns: { success: Boolean, deletedId: String, alreadyDeleted: Boolean }
notes: Requires an unsuspended signed-in creator or admin, matching REST delete permission.
Deletes through the shared homework mutation so soft-delete and homework audit behavior stay shared.

fields: cards/list view mode persisted in browser storage
detail popup order: description, due summary, vertical metadata excluding platform createdAt, edit/completion controls, inline discussion; desktop places discussion to the right of the details
section cards use a responsive multi-column layout
create and edit forms expose the same collapsible advisory homework title and description style guide

notes: Accepts page and pageSize; deprecated limit remains an alias for pageSize.

mutations.name: setHomeworkCompletions
notes: Accepts 1-100 unique homework IDs and preserves REST per-item partial-success semantics.
Missing or deleted homework IDs are reported on their result item without aborting valid updates.

routes.returns: { results: Array<{ success: Boolean, homeworkId: String, completed: Boolean, completedAt?: DateTime?, error?: { code: String, message: String } }> }
notes: Batch completion updates are partial by item: missing or deleted homework IDs return per-item errors while valid items still update.
MCP remains single-item through set_my_homework_completion; this REST batch route exists for HTTP clients that otherwise fan out single-item requests.

tools.name: set_my_homework_completion
tools.returns: { success: Boolean, completion: { homeworkId: String, completed: Boolean, completedAt: DateTime? } }
notes: Pass completed=true to mark as done, completed=false to revert to incomplete.
